Operation 'getOneAttachment' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details. The original summary 'Get one attachment' is too generic and doesn't specify the retrieval mechanism. The description mentions base64 encoding but lacks clarity about what is returned and when to use this operation. The improved version specifies the dual-ID requirement, clarifies the base64 encoding context, and adds actionable guidance for AI agents about the operation's purpose and use case.

data.current_description: Get one message attachment by message id and attachment id from the attachments list. Content response field is encoded in base64 per RFC 4648.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve a specific email attachment by ID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a single attachment from an email message using the message ID and attachment ID. The attachment content is returned in the response body encoded in base64 format per RFC 4648. Use this operation when you need to access the full content of a specific attachment from a received email message.
data.reasoning: The original summary 'Get one attachment' is too generic and doesn't specify the retrieval mechanism. The description mentions base64 encoding but lacks clarity about what is returned and when to use this operation. The improved version specifies the dual-ID requirement, clarifies the base64 encoding context, and adds actionable guidance for AI agents about the operation's purpose and use case.

message: Operation 'getEmails' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary 'Get emails' is too generic. Description lacks clarity about the return value structure and important context about automatic message deletion. Enhanced version specifies the return type, clarifies the MD5 hash requirement, and includes the auto-deletion behavior for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Check and get a list of emails for a mailbox. The md5 parameter is the MD5 hash of the email address (login + domain, e.g. md5('testuser@domain.com')).
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all emails received in a temporary mailbox identified by the MD5 hash of the email address. The md5 parameter should be the MD5 hash of the complete email address (login + domain, e.g., md5('testuser@domain.com')). Returns a list of EmailMessage objects containing message metadata and content. Messages are automatically deleted within 1-2 hours.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Get emails' is too generic. Description lacks clarity about the return value structure and important context about automatic message deletion. Enhanced version specifies the return type, clarifies the MD5 hash requirement, and includes the auto-deletion behavior for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'getOneMessage' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary is too vague. Description lacks context about what mail_id is, what is returned, and when this operation would be used. Enhanced version clarifies the parameter meaning, specifies the return type, and provides use case context for AI agents.

data.current_description: Get one message by its unique identifier.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve single email message by ID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a single email message by its unique mail_id identifier. The mail_id parameter is the system-assigned unique identifier for the message. Returns an EmailMessage object containing the complete message details including sender, subject, body, and any attachments. This operation is useful for retrieving full message content after discovering message IDs from the mailbox list.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too vague. Description lacks context about what mail_id is, what is returned, and when this operation would be used. Enhanced version clarifies the parameter meaning, specifies the return type, and provides use case context for AI agents.

message: Operation 'getMessageSource' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary 'Source message' is unclear and not action-oriented. Description lacks context about what 'raw source' means and what is returned. Enhanced version clarifies the format (RFC 5322), specifies the return type, and explains use cases for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Get the raw source of a message by its mail_id.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the raw email source (RFC 5322 format) for a message identified by its mail_id. Returns a MessageSource object containing the complete SMTP message source including headers and body. This is useful for accessing the original email format, debugging email issues, or extracting specific header information not available in the parsed message object.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Source message' is unclear and not action-oriented. Description lacks context about what 'raw source' means and what is returned. Enhanced version clarifies the format (RFC 5322), specifies the return type, and explains use cases for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'deleteMessage' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary lacks action clarity. Description is missing information about the return value and important side effects (permanent deletion, automatic cleanup). Enhanced version clarifies the permanent nature of deletion, specifies the return type, and provides context about automatic message expiration.

data.current_description: Delete a message by its unique identifier assigned by the system.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a single email message identified by its mail_id. The mail_id parameter is the system-assigned unique identifier for the message. Returns a DeleteResult object confirming successful deletion. This operation immediately removes the message from the mailbox and cannot be undone. Note that messages are automatically deleted within 1-2 hours regardless of manual deletion.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks action clarity. Description is missing information about the return value and important side effects (permanent deletion, automatic cleanup). Enhanced version clarifies the permanent nature of deletion, specifies the return type, and provides context about automatic message expiration.

message: Operation 'getMessageAttachmentsLegacy' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary is vague about the legacy status. Description lacks information about return type and guidance on endpoint selection. Enhanced version clarifies the return type, explicitly recommends the newer endpoint, and explains the difference for AI agent routing decisions.

data.current_description: Get message attachments by message id. This is the legacy endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all attachments for a message identified by its mail_id using the legacy endpoint. Returns a list of Attachment objects containing attachment metadata. This endpoint is deprecated; use getMessageAttachments instead for base64-encoded attachment content. The legacy endpoint may have different response formatting or encoding compared to the current endpoint.
data.reasoning: Original summary is vague about the legacy status. Description lacks information about return type and guidance on endpoint selection. Enhanced version clarifies the return type, explicitly recommends the newer endpoint, and explains the difference for AI agent routing decisions.

message: Operation 'getMessageAttachments' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description mentions base64 encoding but lacks context about the return type structure, how to use the data, and clarification that this is the recommended endpoint. Enhanced version specifies the return type, explains the base64 encoding purpose, and provides guidance for AI agents on data handling.

data.current_description: Get message attachments by message id. Content response field is encoded in base64 per RFC 4648.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all attachments for a message identified by its mail_id. Returns a list of AttachmentWithContent objects where each attachment includes metadata (filename, MIME type, size) and the attachment content encoded in base64 format per RFC 4648. Use this endpoint to access complete attachment data; decode the base64 content field to retrieve the original file bytes. This is the current recommended endpoint for retrieving message attachments.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description mentions base64 encoding but lacks context about the return type structure, how to use the data, and clarification that this is the recommended endpoint. Enhanced version specifies the return type, explains the base64 encoding purpose, and provides guidance for AI agents on data handling.
